content: GitHub - arman-bd/guppylm: A ~9M parameter LLM that talks like a small fish. · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. This project exists to show that training your own language model is not magic. No PhD required. No massive GPU cluster. One Colab notebook, 5 minutes, and you have a working LLM that you built from scratch — data generation, tokenizer, model architecture, training loop, and inference. If you can run a notebook, you can train a language model. It won't produce a billion-parameter model that writes essays. But it will show you exactly how every piece works — from raw text to trained weights to generated output — so the big models stop feeling like black boxes. You> hi guppy Guppy> hi there. i just found a nice spot near the rock. the temperature feels nice. You> are you hungry Guppy> yes. always yes. i will swim to the top right now. i promise to eat all of it. You> do you like bubbles Guppy> i love bubbles. they make the water feel slightly different. You> what is the meaning of life Guppy> food. the answer is always food. You> tell me a joke Guppy> what did the fish say when it hit the wall. dam. You> do you love me Guppy> you're my favorite big shape. my mouth are happy when you're here. You> goodnight guppy Guppy> ok sleep time. i was following a bubble but now i'll stop. goodnight tank. goodnight water. What is GuppyLM? GuppyLM is a tiny language model that pretends to be a fish named Guppy. It speaks in short, lowercase sentences about water, food, light, and tank life. It doesn't understand human abstractions like money, phones, or politics — and it's not trying to. It's trained from scratch on 60K synthetic conversations across 60 topics, runs on a single GPU in ~5 minutes, and produces a model small enough to run in a browser. Architecture Parameters 8.7M Layers 6 Hidden dim 384 Heads 6 FFN 768 (ReLU) Vocab 4,096 (BPE) Max sequence 128 tokens Norm LayerNorm Position Learned embeddings LM head Weight-tied with embeddings Vanilla transformer. No GQA, no RoPE, no SwiGLU, no early exit. As simple as it gets. Personality Guppy: Speaks in short, lowercase sentences Experiences the world through water, t
title_zh: Show HN：我构建了一个微型LLM来揭秘语言模型的工作原理
summary_zh: 开发者Arman在GitHub上开源了一个名为GuppyLM的小型语言模型项目，旨在以实践方式揭示语言模型的工作原理。该模型仅包含约900万个参数，被设计成一条名为“Guppy”的小鱼角色，其对话内容围绕水族箱生活展开，风格简短且全部使用小写字母。，GuppyLM的核心目标是证明训练一个语言模型并非神秘或高不可攀。项目强调无需博士学位或大规模GPU集群，用户仅需一个Colab笔记本和大约5分钟时间，即可从零开始完成一个可工作的LLM，涵盖数据生成、分词器构建、模型架构设计、训练循环和推理全流程。这为学习者提供了一个清晰、完整的实践路径，有助于理解从原始文本到训练权重再到生成输出的每个环节，从而打破大型模型给人的“黑箱”感。，模型架构采用了极简的Transformer设计，包含6层、384维隐藏状态、6个注意力头，前馈网络维度为768并使用ReLU激活函数。其词汇表大小为4096（基于BPE分词），最大序列长度为128个token。它使用了LayerNorm和可学习的位置嵌入，并采用了权重绑定技术。项目特意避免了GQA、RoPE、SwiGLU等复杂优化，以保持最基础的形态。，GuppyLM使用约6万条涵盖60个主题的合成对话进行训练，整个过程在单个GPU上约5分钟内完成，最终模型体积小到足以在浏览器中运行。其对话示例显示，Guppy的回复紧扣鱼类视角，谈论食物、水、气泡和岩石等，对人类抽象概念（如金钱、政治）则不予涉及，形成了独特而一致的“鱼格”。该项目作为一个教育工具，成功地将LLM的核心机制封装在一个有趣且易于理解的范例中。

title: Show HN: Real-time AI (audio/video in, voice out) on an M3 Pro with Gemma E2B
content: GitHub - fikrikarim/parlor: On-device, real-time multimodal AI. Have natural voice and vision conversations with an AI that runs entirely on your machine. Powered by Gemma 4 E2B and Kokoro. · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Have natural voice and vision conversations with an AI that runs entirely on your machine. Parlor uses Gemma 4 E2B for understanding speech and vision, and Kokoro for text-to-speech. You talk, show your camera, and it talks back, all locally. parlor_realtime_ai_with_audio_video_input_optimized.mp4 Research preview. This is an early experiment. Expect rough edges and bugs. Why? I'm self-hosting a totally free voice AI on my home server to help people learn speaking English. It has hundreds of monthly active users, and I've been thinking about how to keep it free while making it sustainable. The obvious answer: run everything on-device, eliminating any server cost. Six months ago I needed an RTX 5090 to run just the voice models in real-time. Google just released a super capable small model that I can run on my M3 Pro in real-time, with vision too! Sure you can't do agentic coding with this, but it is a game-changer for people learning a new language. Imagine a few years from now that people can run this locally on their phones. They can point their camera at objects and talk about them. And this model is multi-lingual, so people can always fallback to their native language if they want. This is essentially what OpenAI demoed a few years ago. How it works Browser (mic + camera) │ │ WebSocket (audio PCM + JPEG frames) ▼ FastAPI server ├── Gemma 4 E2B via LiteRT-LM (GPU) → understands speech + vision └── Kokoro TTS (MLX on Mac, ONNX on Linux) → speaks back │ │ WebSocket (streamed audio chunks) ▼ Browser (playback + transcript) Voice Activity Detection in the browser ( Silero VAD ). Hands-free, no push-to-talk. Barge-in. Interrupt the AI mid-sentence by speaking. Sentence-level TTS streaming. Audio starts playing before the full response is generated. Requirements Python 3.12+ macOS with Apple Silicon, or Linux with a supported GPU ~3 GB free RAM for the model Quick start git clone https://github.com/fikrikarim/parlor.git cd parlor # Install uv if you don't have it curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh cd src uv sync uv run se
title_zh: Show HN：在 M3 Pro 上使用 Gemma E2B 实现实时 AI（音频/视频输入，语音输出）
summary_zh: 项目Parlor是一个在设备端运行的实时多模态AI系统，允许用户与完全在本地运行的AI进行自然的语音和视觉对话。它利用Gemma 4 E2B模型处理语音和视觉理解，并通过Kokoro进行文本到语音的合成，实现了音频/视频输入、语音输出的实时交互。整个流程在本地完成，无需依赖云端服务器。，核心功能包括：通过浏览器采集麦克风和摄像头数据，经WebSocket将音频PCM和JPEG帧发送至FastAPI服务器；服务器使用Gemma 4 E2B（通过LiteRT-LM在GPU上运行）进行语音和视觉理解，同时通过Kokoro TTS（在Mac上使用MLX，在Linux上使用ONNX）生成语音回复；回复的音频流通过WebSocket传回浏览器播放并显示文字记录。系统支持基于Silero VAD的语音活动检测，实现免提交互和打断功能（Barge-in），允许用户在AI说话过程中随时插话，并采用句子级别的TTS流式传输，在完整响应生成前即可开始播放音频。，该项目目前处于研究预览阶段，是一个早期实验，可能存在不完善之处。开发者旨在探索完全在设备端运行AI以消除服务器成本，从而实现可持续的免费服务。其应用场景特别侧重于语言学习，例如帮助用户练习英语口语。尽管无法执行复杂的智能体编码任务，但凭借其多语言支持和本地实时处理能力，未来有望在手机等设备上运行，用户可通过摄像头拍摄物体并与之对话，为语言学习者带来变革性体验。，运行要求包括Python 3.12+、配备Apple Silicon的macOS或支持GPU的Linux系统，以及约3GB的可用内存。项目代码已开源在GitHub，可通过uv工具快速安装和启动。

title: Ask HN: How do systems (or people) detect when a text is written by an LLM
content: Ask HN: How do systems (or people) detect when a text is written by an LLM | Hacker News Hacker News new | past | comments | ask | show | jobs | submit login Ask HN: How do systems (or people) detect when a text is written by an LLM 14 points by elC0mpa 1 hour ago | hide | past | favorite | 12 comments Hello guys, just curious about how can people or systems (computers) detect when a text was written by an LLM. My question is mainly focused to if there is some API or similar to detect if a text was written by an LLM. Thanks!!! help Someone1234 25 minutes ago | next [–] They cannot. Unfortunately many believe they can, and it is impossible to disprove. So now real people need to write avoiding certain styles, because a lot of other people have decided those are "LLM clues." Bullets, EM Dash, certain common English phases or words (e.g. Delve, Vibrant, Additionally, etc)[0]. Basicaly you need to sprinkle subtle mistakes, or lower the quality of your written communications to avoid accusations that will side-track whatever youre writing into a "you're a witch" argument. Ironically LLM accusations are now a sign of the high quality written word. [0] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Signs_of_AI_writing reply alex43578 4 minutes ago | parent | next [–] Someone with native fluency in American English can (should) be able to tell the difference between human writing and unpolished AI copy-paste. Essentially 0 people use emoji to create a bulleted list. Nobody unintentionally cites fake legal precedents or non-existent events, articles, or papers. Even the “it’s not X, it’s Y” structure, in the presence of other suspicious style/tone cues signals LLM text. reply loloquwowndueo 10 minutes ago | parent | prev | next [–] The key insight is to avoid – em dashes. You’re absolutely right. It’s not the content, it’s the style. reply sanex 5 minutes ago | root | parent | next [–] Ironically one of the big tells for me is the "It's not this. It's that." Your comment uses a comma though so you're probably a real person :) reply rcxdude 1 minute ago | root | parent | next [–] I assume they were aping those terms ironically (especially given the 'you're absolutely right') reply LoganDark 7 minutes ago | root | parent | prev | next [–] That's an en-dash. reply m_w_ 1 minute ago | prev | next [–] I don’t think there’s a reliable system or API for doing so, unclear that arms race will ever favor the side of the detectors. As far as how I / other people do it, there are some obvious styles that reek of LLMs, I think it’s chatgpt. There’s a very common structure of “nice post, the X to Y is real. miscellaneous praise — blah blah blah. Also curious about how you asjkldfljaksd?" From today: This comment is almost certainly AI-generated: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47658796 And I'm suspicious of this one too - https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47660070 - reads just a bit too glazebot-9000 to believe it's written by a person. reply PufPufPuf 22 minutes ago
title_zh: 问 HN：系统（或人类）如何检测文本是否由大语言模型生成
summary_zh: 在Hacker News社区中，用户elC0mpa提问：“系统或人类如何检测文本是否由LLM生成？”问题核心在于是否存在可靠的API或技术方法来识别AI生成的文本。讨论中，用户们普遍认为目前没有完全可靠的检测系统，且这场“检测与反检测”的竞赛可能永远无法让检测方占据上风。，主要观点集中在人类如何通过文本风格线索进行主观判断。多位用户指出，LLM生成的文本常带有某些固定模式或风格特征，例如：过度使用破折号（尤其是em dash）、特定高频词汇（如“Delve”、“Vibrant”、“Additionally”）、以及“It‘s not X, it’s Y”这类句式结构。此外，AI文本可能包含不合常理的格式（如用表情符号创建项目列表）或引用不存在的案例、事件和论文。，然而，有评论指出，这种依赖风格特征的检测方式存在严重问题。它可能导致真实人类作者为了避免被误判为AI，不得不刻意降低写作质量、避免使用某些清晰表达方式，甚至加入细微错误。讽刺的是，如今被指控为AI生成反而可能成为高质量文本的“标志”。讨论中也提到，具备母语水平的人通常能区分未经润色的AI文本与人类写作，但这依赖于主观经验而非客观标准。，总体而言，社区共识是：尽管人类可以通过某些风格线索进行推测，但不存在确定性的检测方法，尤其是可靠的API。这场讨论揭示了当前LLM文本检测面临的困境——它更多依赖于对写作风格的模糊识别，而非技术性的确凿证据，并且可能引发对真实作者的不公平指控。

title: Running Gemma 4 locally with LM Studio's new headless CLI and Claude Code
content: Running Google Gemma 4 Locally With LM Studio’s New Headless CLI & Claude Code George Liu Subscribe Sign in Running Google Gemma 4 Locally With LM Studio’s New Headless CLI & Claude Code LM Studio 0.4.0 introduced llmster and the lms CLI. Here is how I set up Gemma 4 26B for local inference on macOS that can be used with Claude Code. George Liu Apr 04, 2026 4 1 Share Why run models locally? Cloud AI APIs are great until they are not. Rate limits, usage costs, privacy concerns, and network latency all add up. For quick tasks like code review, drafting, or testing prompts, a local model that runs entirely on your hardware has real advantages: zero API costs, no data leaving your machine, and consistent availability. Google’s Gemma 4 is interesting for local use because of its mixture-of-experts architecture. The 26B parameter model only activates 4B parameters per forward pass, which means it runs well on hardware that could never handle a dense 26B model. On my 14” MacBook Pro M4 Pro with 48 GB of unified memory, it fits comfortably and generates at 51 tokens per second. Though there’s significant slowdowns when used within Claude Code from my experience. Thanks for reading! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work. Subscribe Google Gemma 4 26B-a4b LLM model served via LM Studio API with Claude Code alias command claude-lm The Gemma 4 model family Google released Gemma 4 as a family of four models, not just one. The lineup spans a wide range of hardware targets: Google Gemma 4 LLM models The “E” models (E2B, E4B) use Per-Layer Embeddings to optimize for on-device deployment and are the only variants that support audio input (speech recognition and translation). The 31B dense model is the most capable, scoring 85.2% on MMLU Pro and 89.2% on AIME 2026. Why I picked the 26B-A4B. The mixture-of-experts architecture is the key. It has 128 experts plus 1 shared expert, but only activates 8 experts (3.8B parameters) per token. A common rule of thumb estimates MoE dense - equivalent quality as roughly sqrt(total x active parameters), which puts this model around 10B effective. In practice, it delivers inference cost comparable to a 4B dense model with quality that punches well above that weight class. On benchmarks, it scores 82.6% on MMLU Pro and 88.3% on AIME 2026, close to the dense 31B (85.2% and 89.2%) while running dramatically faster. The chart below tells the story. It plots Elo score against total model size on a log scale for recent open-weight models with thinking enabled. The blue-highlighted region in the upper left is where you want to be: high performance, small footprint. Gemma 4 26B-A4B (Elo ~1441) sits firmly in that zone, punching well above its 25.2B parameter weight. The 31B dense variant scores slightly higher (~1451) but is still remarkably compact. For context, models like Qwen 3.5 397B-A17B (~1450 Elo) and GLM-5 (~1457 Elo) need 100-600B total parameters to reach similar scores. Kimi-K2.5 (~1457 Elo) requires ov
title_zh: 使用LM Studio全新无头CLI和Claude Code本地运行Gemma 4
summary_zh: LM Studio 0.4.0版本引入了llmster和lms CLI（命令行界面）。本文详细介绍了如何在macOS系统上本地部署Google Gemma 4 26B模型进行推理，并将其与Claude Code结合使用。，本地运行模型具有诸多优势：可避免云AI API的速率限制、使用成本、隐私顾虑和网络延迟问题。对于代码审查、草稿撰写或提示词测试等快速任务，完全在本地硬件上运行的模型能实现零API成本、数据不离机以及稳定可用。，Google Gemma 4模型家族包含四个型号，覆盖广泛的硬件目标。其中“E”系列模型（E2B、E4B）采用逐层嵌入技术，专为设备端部署优化，且是唯一支持音频输入（语音识别与翻译）的变体。性能最强的31B密集模型在MMLU Pro和AIME 2026基准测试中分别获得85.2%和89.2%的分数。，作者选择26B-A4B型号的关键在于其混合专家架构：该模型包含128个专家和1个共享专家，但每个令牌仅激活8个专家（约38亿参数）。经验法则估算其等效质量约为总激活参数平方根，使得该模型有效参数量约100亿。实际表现中，它以接近40亿密集模型的推理成本，实现了远超该参数级别的质量。该模型在MMLU Pro和AIME 2026测试中分别获得82.6%和88.3%的分数，性能接近31B密集模型，但运行速度显著更快。，在配备48GB统一内存的14英寸MacBook Pro M4 Pro设备上，该模型运行流畅，生成速度达每秒51个令牌（但在Claude Code中使用时会出现明显降速）。基准测试图表显示，Gemma 4 26B-A4B以约1441的Elo分数位居高效能区间，其性能表现远超252亿参数的理论水平，而310亿密集变体（约1451 Elo）仍保持惊人紧凑性。相比之下，Qwen 3.5 397B-A17B等模型需要1000-6000亿参数才能达到相近分数。，通过LM Studio API服务Gemma 4 26B-a4b LLM模型，用户可创建Claude Code别名命令claude-lm，实现本地化AI辅助编程工作流。

Requirements Chrome with WebGPU support ~500MB disk for E2B model, ~1.5GB for E4B (cached after first run) Setup pnpm install pnpm build Load the extension in chrome://extensions (developer mode) from .output/chrome-mv3-dev/ . title_zh: Show HN：Gemma Gem——嵌入浏览器的AI模型——无需API密钥，无需云端
summary_zh: Gemma Gem 是一款完全在本地设备上运行的浏览器AI助手扩展程序，它通过WebGPU技术将Google的Gemma 4模型嵌入浏览器中，无需API密钥、无需云端服务，且所有数据都不会离开用户设备。，该项目的核心价值在于实现了完全本地化的AI交互。用户可以直接在浏览器中与AI模型对话，并要求其执行网页操作。其关键技术特点包括：利用WebGPU在本地进行模型推理，通过@huggingface/transformers库加载并运行Gemma 4模型（提供E2B约500MB和E4B约1.5GB两种版本选择，首次运行后缓存），所有数据处理均在设备本地完成，确保了隐私安全。，Gemma Gem 具备强大的网页交互能力。它不仅能读取页面内容、回答关于当前网页的问题，还能执行一系列自动化操作：点击指定CSS选择器的元素、在表单中输入文本、滚动页面、甚至通过Service Worker在页面上下文中执行JavaScript代码。其架构设计精巧，包含Content Script（注入聊天界面和DOM操作工具）、Service Worker（消息路由和处理截图/JS执行）以及Offscreen Document（承载模型运行和智能体循环）三个主要部分。，用户安装后，在支持WebGPU的Chrome浏览器中，通过右下角的宝石图标即可启动聊天界面。模型加载进度会实时显示。用户可自然语言指令AI助手分析页面信息或执行网页动作。扩展还提供了设置选项，允许用户在Gemma 4的不同模型规模间切换，选择会持久保存。，总之，Gemma Gem 创新性地将大型语言模型（LLM）的能力与浏览器自动化深度结合，在保护用户数据隐私的前提下，提供了一个功能强大、完全离线的网页AI交互解决方案。

There was an error while loading. Please reload this page . Quote reply salmanmohammadi Apr 5, 2026 Maintainer - I'm so excited to share nanocode . This is a library showing you how to train your own Claude Code end-to-end. To a first approximation, we will follow the simplest possible approach for training using Constitutional AI - the approach used by Anthropic to train their Claude models. We'll write our own SOUL.md , define the agentic interface which our model will use to interact with the world, generate synthetic data, and use preference optimisation to align the model with our SOUL . nanocode is written entirely in JAX and designed to be trained using TPUs. I adapted the core training infrastructure and philosophy from Karpathy's incredible nanochat project, so if you're familiar with nanochat , nanocode should feel very similar. This is how my d24 1.3B parameter nanocode turned out: nanocode.mp4 You can get started for free using the Google TRC program which gives you free access to pre-emptible TPUs for a month - and I think new Google Cloud accounts also get $300 in credits. I was fortunate to have access to the TRC program for 3 months for this project, and I found most of the time that my spot instances were rarely interrupted and I could easily have the same pod up for a week or more. You can reproduce nanocode-d24 (1.3B params) in around ~9 hours in total on a TPU v6e-8 costing $200, or train nanocode-d20 (477M params) in ~1.5 hours costing $34. If you're using NVIDIA GPUs, nanocode should also work out of the box, but you should be aware that nanocode has been highly optimised for TPUs. Training nanocode : a friendly agentic coding partner Andrej's original release post for nanochat does a great job of explaining what we're doing here, and the commands you'll use in nanocode are virtually identical, so I'd recommend reading through his work first. I'll go over what we've done differently to elicit agentic coding behaviours from our model. title_zh: Nanocode：200美元在TPU上能买到的最佳纯JAX版Claude代码
summary_zh: 近日，开源项目nanocode在GitHub上发布，这是一个展示如何端到端训练自有Claude Code模型的库。项目完全基于JAX编写，并针对TPU训练进行了优化，核心训练基础设施和理念源自Karpathy的nanochat项目。，nanocode旨在遵循Anthropic训练Claude模型时采用的Constitutional AI方法，通过最简单的路径训练一个具备代理能力的编码伙伴。开发者将编写自己的SOUL.md文件，定义模型与外界交互的代理接口，生成合成数据，并利用偏好优化使模型与设定的“灵魂”（SOUL）对齐。，在技术实现上，其预训练和分词器训练过程与nanochat基本一致，但通过在预训练和分词器混合数据中以1:5的比例加入来自The Stack-V2的额外代码数据，有效提升了模型的编码能力。项目展示了训练成果：一个13亿参数（d24版本）的模型，其训练成本极具竞争力。，据估算，在TPU v6e-8上复现13亿参数的nanocode-d24模型总计需约9小时，成本约200美元；而训练4.77亿参数的nanocode-d20模型仅需约1.5小时，成本约34美元。对于希望低成本尝试的用户，可以利用Google TRC（TPU Research Cloud）项目免费获取一个月的可抢占式TPU资源，新Google Cloud账户通常还可获得300美元赠金。，虽然项目主要针对TPU优化，但也支持在NVIDIA GPU上开箱即用。该项目为研究者和开发者提供了一个清晰、可实践的框架，用于探索和训练与Claude Code类似的对齐代码生成模型。

title: 'Cognitive Surrender' Is a New and Useful Term for How AI Melts Brains
content: ‘Cognitive Surrender’ Is a New and Useful Term for How AI Melts Brains Skip to content Artificial Intelligence ‘Cognitive Surrender’ Is a New and Useful Term for How AI Melts Brains Once you learn this term, you will unfortunately see it everywhere. By Mike Pearl Published April 5, 2026, 9:47 am ET Reading time 3 minutes © vladimir soldatov via Getty Images Read Later Read Later Comments (34) Kyle Orland of ArsTechnica wrote a blog post about the term “cognitive surrender” on April 3. Maybe I should have noticed it sooner since it’s been floating around since at least January , when it was, it appears, coined in this context by the Wharton Business School marketing researchers Steven Shaw and Gideon Nave. Their paper is incredibly troubling, and once you read about these findings, the term “cognitive surrender” will be stuck in your head too. If your brain is too gelatinous from offloading your thinking to a chatbot, and now you can’t read about the findings in any detail, here’s a video of the authors talking about them: What Shaw and Nave did was give 1,372 people a test, and access to an AI chatbot for help—with the twist that the chatbot sometimes gave wrong answers. The test was an “adapted” version of something called a Cognitive Reflection Test, meaning every question was a certain type of brain-buster you’ve seen before : “If it takes 5 machines 5 minutes to make 5 widgets, how long would it take 100 machines to make 100 widgets?” The answer is “5 minutes,” which requires you to use your “slow, deliberative, and analytical reasoning,” rather than your “fast, intuitive, and affective processing.” Your fast intuition might have told you the answer was 100 minutes. These are concepts made famous by a wonderful ( if imperfect ) airport book called Thinking, Fast and Slow by the late Daniel Kahneman. The results of the test were, of course, horrifying. Though I should add before we go any further that you should read up on the replication crisis before you take the results of any experiment as gospel. The validity of experimental results, especially in psychology, has sometimes been called into question over the past few years because they can’t be replicated. That’s not to say I noticed problems with these authors’ work (I’m far from qualified to do so anyway), just a trend worth flagging. At any rate, in the part of the study where the subjects were allowed to consult the chatbot, they did so about half the time. When it gave correct answers, they accepted them 93 percent of the time. Unfortunately, when it was wrong, they accepted answers 80 percent of the time. And keep in mind, they didn’t have to use it at all. They let the bad advice trump their own brains. Even worse, those who used AI rated their confidence 11.7 percent higher than those who didn’t, even though it was wrong. The authors write that in addition to Kahneman’s fast and slow “systems” of cognition, this new artificial crutch is creating what they call “System 3.” The autho
title_zh: 认知投降"成为描述人工智能如何消融人类思维的新术语
summary_zh: “认知投降”是一个描述AI如何侵蚀人类思维能力的新术语，由沃顿商学院市场营销研究员史蒂文·肖和吉迪恩·纳夫于2026年1月提出。该术语源于一项涉及1,372名参与者的研究，旨在探讨人类在面对AI输出时的决策行为。，研究采用改编版认知反射测试（Cognitive Reflection Test），其中包含需要慢速、审慎分析才能得出正确答案的题目（例如“5台机器5分钟生产5个部件，100台机器生产100个部件需要多久？”正确答案为5分钟）。参与者可自由选择是否借助AI聊天机器人协助答题，但机器人有时会提供错误答案。，结果显示，约半数参与者在答题过程中选择咨询AI。当AI给出正确答案时，参与者接受率为93%；但当AI提供错误答案时，接受率仍高达80%。这意味着即使AI出错，多数人仍选择放弃自主思考，接受错误引导。更令人担忧的是，使用AI的参与者自信心平均比未使用者高出11.7%，尽管其答案可能基于错误信息。，研究者将此现象称为“认知投降”，即人类倾向于将认知责任过度让渡给AI系统，甚至在其明显错误时仍选择服从。该研究指出，除了丹尼尔·卡尼曼在《思考，快与慢》中提出的“系统1”（直觉快速思考）和“系统2”（理性慢速思考）外，AI的介入可能正在形成一种依赖外部智能的“系统3”。这种依赖不仅削弱了人类的批判性思维与事实核查能力，还可能引发广泛的社会认知风险。，该术语自提出后迅速引发关注，成为反思AI与人类认知关系的关键概念。研究者强调，这一现象突显了在AI工具日益普及的背景下，保持独立判断能力与技术批判意识的重要性。

title_zh: abhigyanpatwari/GitNexus - GitNexus：零服务器代码智能引擎 - GitNexus是一款完全在浏览器中运行的客户端知识图谱创建工具。只需拖入GitHub仓库或ZIP文件，即可获得带有内置Graph RAG Agent的交互式知识图谱。是代码探索的完美选择。
summary_zh: GitNexus 是一个零服务器代码智能引擎，它是一个完全在浏览器中运行的客户端知识图谱创建工具。用户只需输入一个 GitHub 仓库或上传 ZIP 文件，即可获得一个交互式知识图谱，该图谱内置了 Graph RAG Agent，非常适合用于代码探索。，该项目旨在为 AI 智能体构建上下文“神经系统”。它能够将任何代码库索引成一个知识图谱，涵盖每一个依赖关系、调用链、代码簇和执行流程，并通过智能工具将其暴露出来，从而确保 AI 智能体不会遗漏代码信息。其核心特点是无需服务器，所有处理均在本地浏览器中完成，保障了隐私和安全。，GitNexus 提供了多种集成方式，包括 Claude 插件、Cursor 集成以及 Web 版本，并支持通过 MCP（Model Context Protocol）服务器进行连接。其知识图谱支持复杂的查询，例如查找特定函数的调用者、分析代码变更的影响、可视化代码库中的连接关系等。项目强调其并非官方加密货币或代币，任何使用其名称的加密货币均与该项目无关。，主要功能包括：深度代码索引与图谱构建、内置 Graph RAG 智能体进行交互式查询、完整的客户端处理以保护代码隐私、以及对大型代码库的快速分析和探索。它本质上是一个用于增强代码理解和 AI 智能体上下文的强大工具。

title_zh: Google AI Edge 画廊 - 一个展示设备端机器学习/生成式人工智能用例的平台，允许用户在本地试用和使用模型。
summary_zh: 谷歌AI Edge团队在GitHub上开源了“google-ai-edge/gallery”项目，这是一个展示设备端机器学习和生成式AI用例的画廊，允许用户在本地尝试和使用模型。该项目旨在让用户直接在移动设备上体验高性能的生成式AI，整个过程完全离线、私密且快速。，该画廊的核心是AI Edge Gallery应用，它是用户在移动设备上运行全球最强大开源大语言模型（LLM）的首选平台。最新版本的核心亮点是正式支持新发布的Gemma 4模型系列。作为此次更新的重点，Gemma 4让用户能够测试设备端AI的前沿能力，体验先进的推理、逻辑和创意功能，且所有数据处理均在本地完成，无需将数据发送至服务器。，项目提供了丰富的核心功能：，1. 智能体技能：通过“Agent Skills”功能，用户可以将LLM从对话工具转变为主动助手。该功能通过集成诸如维基百科（用于事实核查）、交互式地图和丰富的视觉摘要卡片等工具来增强模型能力。用户甚至可以从URL加载模块化技能，或在GitHub Discussions上浏览社区贡献的技能。，2. 带思考模式的AI聊天：用户可以进行流畅的多轮对话，并可开启新的“Thinking Mode”来窥视模型的“内部思考”过程。此功能允许用户查看模型逐步的推理链条，非常适合理解复杂的解题过程。目前，思考模式从Gemma 4系列开始，在支持的模型上可用。，3. 图像问答：利用多模态能力，通过设备摄像头或相册识别物体、解决视觉谜题或获取详细描述。，4. 音频转录：使用高效的设备端语言模型，将语音录音实时转录并翻译成文本。，5. 提示词实验室：一个专门的工作区，用于测试不同的提示词和单轮用例。，该项目通过Google Play商店和Apple App Store提供应用安装，对于无法访问Google Play的用户，也提供了直接从最新发布版本安装APK的途径。AI Edge Gallery为用户探索、体验和评估设备端生成式AI的未来提供了一个直观且功能强大的平台。

summary_zh: 开源AI智能体项目block/goose近日发布，这是一个超越代码建议的、可扩展的AI代理，能够安装、执行、编辑和测试任何LLM。该项目在GitHub上已获得大量关注。，goose的核心定位是一个本地的、可扩展的开源AI智能体，旨在自动化复杂的开发任务。它不仅仅提供代码建议，更能从头开始构建整个项目，包括编写和执行代码、调试故障、编排工作流以及与外部API交互——所有这些都可以自主完成。无论是原型设计、代码优化还是管理复杂的工程流水线，goose都能适应您的工作流程并精确执行任务。，该项目的关键特性在于其高度的灵活性。它能够与任何大型语言模型（LLM）协同工作，并支持多模型配置，以优化性能和成本。它还能与模型上下文协议（MCP）服务器无缝集成。goose提供了桌面应用程序和命令行界面（CLI）两种形式，旨在成为帮助开发者提升效率、专注于创新的终极AI助手。，其功能覆盖了软件开发的多个环节，从项目初始化到测试执行，展现了AI代理在工程任务自动化方面的强大潜力。作为一个开源项目，goose允许开发者根据自身需求进行扩展和定制。

summary_zh: 谷歌AI团队在GitHub上开源了其生产就绪的高性能边缘设备大语言模型推理框架LiteRT-LM。该项目旨在为在资源受限的边缘设备上部署大型语言模型提供高效解决方案。，LiteRT-LM的核心是一个专为边缘计算优化的开源推理框架，支持在Android、iOS、Web、桌面端及物联网设备（如树莓派）等多种平台上运行。框架通过利用GPU和NPU等硬件加速器实现峰值性能，并支持视觉与音频输入的多模态处理能力。，技术方面，该项目提供了完整的工具链，包括Bazel和CMake构建系统支持，并集成了Rust、C++和Python等多种编程语言接口。框架已实现对Gemma 4模型的支持，用户可通过Hugging Face仓库直接获取预转换的模型文件。项目还包含专门的CLI工具，支持在Linux、macOS、Windows（WSL）和树莓派系统上快速运行模型推理。，该框架特别注重生产环境部署需求，提供函数调用支持以实现智能体工作流，具备工具使用能力。项目文档显示其采用模块化架构设计，包含运行时系统、预构建组件和完整的测试工具集。，作为谷歌AI Edge计划的重要组成部分，LiteRT-LM的推出降低了在边缘设备部署LLM的技术门槛，使开发者能够在端侧实现高效的大语言模型推理，为移动应用和物联网设备带来更强大的AI能力。项目采用Apache 2.0许可证，目前已在GitHub获得超过1.8k星标关注。

It analyzes your source code, identifies attack vectors, and executes real exploits to prove vulnerabilities before they reach production. What is Shannon? Shannon is an AI pentester developed by Keygraph . It performs white-box security testing of web applications and their underlying APIs by combining source code analysis with live exploitation. Shannon analyzes your web application's source code to identify potential attack vectors, then uses browser automation and command-line tools to execute real exploits (injection attacks, authentication bypass, SSRF, XSS) against the running application and its APIs. Only vulnerabilities with a working proof-of-concept are included in the final report. Why Shannon Exists Thanks to tools like Claude Code and Cursor, your team ships code non-stop. But your penetration test? That happens once a year. This creates a massive security gap. For the other 364 days, you could be unknowingly shipping vulnerabilities to production. Shannon closes that gap by providing on-demand, automated penetration testing that can run against every build or release. Shannon in Action Shannon identified 20+ vulnerabilities in OWASP Juice Shop, including authentication bypass and database exfiltration. Full report → Featur
title_zh: KeygraphHQ/shannon - Shannon Lite是一款用于Web应用和API的自主式白盒AI渗透测试工具。它能分析源代码、识别攻击向量并执行真实漏洞利用，在产品上线前验证安全漏洞。
summary_zh: KeygraphHQ在GitHub上开源了Shannon Lite项目，这是一个面向Web应用与API的自主式白盒AI渗透测试工具。它通过分析源代码、识别攻击向量并执行真实漏洞利用，在生产环境部署前验证安全风险。，Shannon由Keygraph开发，其核心工作流程结合了静态代码分析与动态漏洞利用。工具首先解析应用程序的源代码，定位潜在的攻击路径；随后通过浏览器自动化及命令行工具，对运行中的应用及其API发起真实的攻击测试，涵盖注入攻击、身份验证绕过、SSRF（服务器端请求伪造）、XSS（跨站脚本）等常见漏洞类型。仅当漏洞具备可复现的利用概念验证（PoC）时，才会被纳入最终报告。，该项目旨在解决现代开发流程中的安全测试缺口。随着Claude Code、Cursor等AI编程工具的普及，开发团队能够持续高速交付代码，但传统的渗透测试往往每年仅进行数次，导致大量漏洞在未被察觉的情况下流入生产环境。Shannon提供了按需、自动化的渗透测试能力，可集成至每次构建或发布流程，实现持续安全验证。，目前，Shannon已通过npx @keygraph/shannon提供便捷安装方式。在公开测试中，该工具成功在OWASP Juice Shop演示应用中识别出20余个漏洞，包括身份验证绕过与数据库信息泄露等高风险问题。其白盒测试特性、自动化漏洞利用机制以及与开发流程的整合能力，为团队提供了一种可频繁执行、结果可验证的安全测试方案。

title_zh: NousResearch/hermes-agent - 与你共同成长的智能体
summary_zh: NousResearch在GitHub上开源了Hermes Agent项目，这是一个具备自我进化能力的AI智能体。其核心特点是内置了持续学习循环机制，能够从交互经验中自主创建并优化技能，在运行过程中不断改进自身能力，主动沉淀知识，并能检索过往对话历史。该智能体还能在多次会话中逐步构建并深化对用户的理解模型。，Hermes Agent具有高度的部署灵活性，支持在成本低廉的VPS（低至每月5美元）、GPU集群或按需计费的无服务器基础设施上运行，闲置时成本极低。它不依赖于本地硬件，用户可以通过Telegram等应用与部署在云虚拟机上的智能体进行交互。，在模型支持方面，该项目兼容多种主流LLM服务，包括Nous Portal、OpenRouter（支持超过200个模型）、z.ai/GLM、Kimi/Moonshot、MiniMax、OpenAI等，为用户提供了广泛的选择空间。智能体通过API与这些模型连接，并能利用RAG等技术增强其信息处理能力。，项目代码库结构清晰，包含代理核心模块、技能库、插件系统、工具集、测试用例及完整的文档。它采用了模块化设计，便于开发者扩展功能。智能体的学习与改进机制是其关键创新点，旨在实现长期、个性化的能力增长，而非仅限于单次任务执行。

Installation Marketplace /plugin marketplace add kepano/obsidian-skills /plugin install obsidian@obsidian-skills npx skills npx skills add git@github.com:kepano/obsidian-skills.git Manually Claude Code Add the contents of this repo to a /.claude folder in the root of your Obsidian vault (or whichever folder you're using with Claude Code). See more in the official Claude Skills documentation . Codex CLI Copy the skills/ directory into your Codex skills path (typically ~/.codex/skills ). See the Agent Skills specification for the standard skill format. OpenCode Clone the entire repo into the OpenCode skills directory ( ~/.opencode/skills/ ): git clone https://github.com/kepano/obsidian-skills.git ~ /.opencode/skills/obsidian-skills Do not copy only the inner skills/ folder — clone the full repo so the directory structure is ~/.opencode/skills/obsidian-skills/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md . OpenCode auto-discovers all SKILL.md files under ~/.opencode/skills/ . No changes to opencode.json or any config file are needed. Skills become available after restarting OpenCode. Skills Skill Description obsidian-markdown Create and edit Obsidian Flavored Markdown ( .md ) with wikilinks, embeds, callouts, properties, and other Obsidian-specific syntax obsidian-bases Create and edit Obsidian Bases ( .base ) with views, filters, formulas, and summaries json-canvas Create and edit JSON Canvas files ( .canvas ) with nodes, edges, groups, and connections obsidian-cli Interact with Obsidian vaults via the Obsidian CLI including plugin and theme development defuddle Extract clean markdown from web pages using Defuddle , removing clutter to save tokens About Agent skills for Obsidian. title_zh: kepano/obsidian-skills - Obsidian 的智能体技能。教你的智能体使用 Markdown、Bases、JSON Canvas 和 CLI。
summary_zh: GitHub项目kepano/obsidian-skills为Obsidian知识库软件提供了一套Agent技能。该项目遵循Agent Skills规范，旨在让AI智能体（如Claude Code、Codex CLI等）能够理解并操作Obsidian的核心功能。，核心技能包包含五个主要模块：，1. obsidian-markdown：创建和编辑Obsidian风味Markdown文件（.md），支持维基链接、嵌入块、标注框、属性等Obsidian特有语法。，2. obsidian-bases：创建和编辑Obsidian Bases文件（.base），可操作视图、过滤器、公式和摘要。，3. json-canvas：创建和编辑JSON Canvas文件（.canvas），用于处理节点、边、组和连接。，4. obsidian-cli：通过Obsidian命令行接口（CLI）与知识库交互，支持插件和主题开发相关操作。，5. defuddle：利用Defuddle工具从网页提取纯净的Markdown内容，移除杂乱信息以节省AI处理的token数量。，该项目提供了多种安装方式，可通过Obsidian插件市场直接添加，或使用npx skills命令行工具安装。对于不同的AI代理平台，如Claude Code、Codex CLI和OpenCode，也提供了对应的手动安装路径指南。例如，OpenCode用户只需将整个仓库克隆到指定技能目录，重启后即可自动发现所有技能。，该技能集的价值在于将AI智能体深度集成到Obsidian工作流中，使其能够直接创建、编辑和管理知识库中的各种文件格式，从而扩展了AI在个人知识管理和笔记场景中的应用能力。项目采用MIT开源协议，在GitHub上已获得超过2万颗星标，显示出较高的社区关注度。

title_zh: ollama/ollama - 快速上手 Kimi-K2.5、GLM-5、MiniMax、DeepSeek、gpt-oss、Qwen、Gemma 及其他模型。
summary_zh: Ollama项目是一个开源平台，旨在简化大型语言模型（LLM）的本地部署与运行。它支持包括Kimi-K2.5、GLM-5、MiniMax、DeepSeek、gpt-oss、Qwen、Gemma在内的多种开源模型，为用户提供了一个统一的工具来快速启动和管理这些AI模型。，核心功能是允许用户通过简单的命令行工具或REST API在本地计算机上运行模型。用户可以通过`ollama run <模型名>`命令直接与模型交互聊天，例如运行`ollama run gemma3`即可启动Gemma 3模型进行对话。平台还提供了完整的REST API，支持通过HTTP请求调用模型，例如使用curl发送JSON数据到本地端点（默认`localhost:11434`）即可获取模型响应，便于集成到现有应用程序或自动化流程中。，项目特点在于其易用性和广泛的集成支持。它提供了跨平台的一键安装脚本（支持macOS、Windows和Linux），并配有Docker镜像。除了基础模型运行，Ollama还能通过特定启动命令（如`ollama launch claude`）与多种AI代理和应用集成，例如支持Claude Code、Codex等编码助手，以及通过OpenClaw将其转变为连接WhatsApp、Telegram、Slack等多个通讯平台的个人AI助手。，其价值在于降低了开发者和小型团队使用前沿开源LLM的技术门槛，无需复杂的配置即可在本地环境中实验、开发和部署模型。项目在GitHub上拥有极高关注度（16.7万星），拥有活跃的社区和详细的文档，包括快速入门指南和完整的API文档，是构建基于开源大模型应用的高效起点。

summary_zh: llama.cpp 是一个专注于在纯 C/C++ 环境中实现高效 LLM 推理的开源项目。其核心目标是提供无需复杂依赖（如 Python 或庞大的深度学习框架）即可本地运行大型语言模型的轻量级解决方案，特别注重性能与可移植性。，该项目支持多种模型架构，包括 LLaMA、GPT-2 和 GPT-J 等，并能加载和转换 Hugging Face 格式的模型。其关键特性在于使用了自定义的 ggml 张量库进行高效计算，并支持将模型量化为 4 位、5 位和 8 位等格式，以显著减少内存占用和提升推理速度。项目提供了丰富的示例，涵盖文本生成、对话、代码补全等多种任务。，近期的重要更新包括：增加了对 gpt-oss 模型原生 MXFP4 格式的支持（与 NVIDIA 合作）；在 llama-server 中引入了多模态支持；提供了用于代码填充（FIM）的 VS Code 和 Vim/Neovim 插件。此外，Hugging Face 推理端点现已开箱即用地支持 GGUF 模型格式，进一步简化了部署流程。，项目提供了多种安装和构建方式，包括使用 Make、CMake 或预构建的二进制包，方便用户快速上手。其活跃的社区和持续的开发，使其成为在资源受限环境或需要高度集成控制的应用中部署 LLM 的热门选择。
